The company is home to artists such as [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC"), [Park Si-eun](/wiki/Park_Si-eun_(entertainer) "Park Si-eun (entertainer)"), and [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

South Korean entertainment company

High Up Entertainment

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Logo used since September 2020 | |
| Native name | 하이업엔터테인먼트 |
| Company type | [Private](/wiki/Privately_held_company "Privately held company") |
| [Traded as](/wiki/Ticker_symbol "Ticker symbol") | Unlisted |
| Industry | [Entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment") |
| Genre | [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") |
| Founded | June 14, 2017; 7 years ago |
| Founder | [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung") |
| Headquarters | 2nd floor, Y Building, 571-13 Yeonnam-dong, Mapo-gu, Seoul 03991 |
| Key people | [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung") |
| [Parent](/wiki/Parent_company "Parent company") | [Kakao Entertainment](/wiki/Kakao_Entertainment "Kakao Entertainment") (40%) |
| Website | [highup-ent.com](http://www.highup-ent.com/) |

**High Up Entertainment** ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 하이업엔터테인먼트), also known as just **High Up**, is a South Korean entertainment company formed under the partnership with [CJ E&M](/wiki/CJ_E%26M "CJ E&M") in 2017. The company is home to artists such as [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC"), [Park Si-eun](/wiki/Park_Si-eun_(entertainer) "Park Si-eun (entertainer)"), and [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=High_Up_Entertainment&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

High Up Entertainment was founded on June 14, 2017, by the producer duo [Black Eyed Pilseung](/wiki/Black_Eyed_Pilseung "Black Eyed Pilseung") in collaboration with [CJ E&M](/wiki/CJ_E%26M "CJ E&M").[[1]](#cite_note-chosun-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) Black Eyed Pilseung has produced songs for multiple [K-pop](/wiki/K-pop "K-pop") artists such as [Twice](/wiki/Twice "Twice"), [Miss A](/wiki/Miss_A "Miss A"), [Apink](/wiki/Apink "Apink"), and [Sistar](/wiki/Sistar "Sistar").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[1]](#cite_note-chosun-1) In January 2018, they held a national talent-scouting audition tour.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[![STAYC in 2020](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/26/STAYC_in_2020_%281%29.png/250px-STAYC_in_2020_%281%29.png)](/wiki/File:STAYC_in_2020_(1).png)

STAYC in 2020

On February 12, 2019, the company debuted their first duo, 415, with the digital single "Remember".[[6]](#cite_note-herald-6)

In December 2019, [Park Si-eun](/wiki/Park_Si-eun_(entertainer) "Park Si-eun (entertainer)") signed a contract with the label and went on to become a member of [STAYC](/wiki/STAYC "STAYC").[[7]](#cite_note-newsen-7)

In October, 2020, they officially announced that the girl group STAYC, who had been previously introduced to the public as "High Up Girls", would debut with the [single album](/wiki/Single_album "Single album") *[Star to a Young Culture](/wiki/Star_to_a_Young_Culture "Star to a Young Culture")* and its [lead single](/wiki/Lead_single "Lead single") "[So Bad](/wiki/So_Bad_(STAYC_song) "So Bad (STAYC song)")" on November 12 after 3 years of preparations.[[7]](#cite_note-newsen-7)[[8]](#cite_note-hankyung-8)

In June 2021, [Kakao Entertainment](/wiki/Kakao_Entertainment "Kakao Entertainment") acquired 40% of the company's shares, becoming its largest shareholder.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The label has been able to gain attention for their group STAYC despite being a small company.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
